description: Domestic companions and household animals — dogs, cats, and other beloved pets.
creatures: Corgi
A small, sturdy herding dog with stubby legs, pointed ears, and an inexhaustible zoomie reserve.
Pug
A wrinkly-faced little dog with bulging eyes and a snoring, snorting, endlessly affectionate personality.
Beagle
A droopy-eared hound that follows its nose into trouble and bays with a voice bigger than its body.
Husky
A thick-furred sled dog with piercing blue eyes that howls at everything and obeys nothing.
Dalmatian
A spotted, high-energy dog that runs alongside carriages and never seems to tire.
Chihuahua
A palm-sized dog with oversized ears and the unshakeable belief that it is the largest creature in the room.
Bulldog
A stocky, flat-faced dog with a courageous heart and a talent for drooling on furniture.
Labrador
A tail-wagging retriever with a gentle mouth that lives to fetch, swim, and please its human.
Poodle
A curly-coated, deceptively athletic dog with sharp intelligence hidden behind a fancy haircut.
Siamese Cat
A sleek, blue-eyed cat with dark-pointed fur and a loud, opinionated yowl.
Maine Coon
A massive, tufted-ear cat with a bushy tail and a gentle, dog-like loyalty to its owner.
Tabby Cat
A striped domestic cat with watchful eyes and a knack for appearing wherever it shouldn't.
Hamster
A tiny, stuffed-cheek ball of fluff that runs endlessly on its wheel and hoards treats.
Goldfish
A shimmering orange fish with flowing fins that drifts in lazy, hypnotic circles.
Parrot
A vivid, sharp-witted bird that mimics voices, solves puzzles, and demands constant attention.
Guinea Pig
A pudgy, squeaking rodent that purrs when petted and popcorn-hops when happy.
Gecko
A tiny, wall-climbing lizard with sticky toe pads and a surprisingly loud chirping call.
Cockatiel
A crested parrot with rosy cheeks that whistles tunes and bobs its head to music.
Canary
A tiny, vibrant songbird whose melodic trills can fill an entire room.
Parakeet
A small, chatty parrot with bright plumage that bonds fiercely to its favorite person.
Chinchilla
A cloud-soft rodent with enormous ears that bounces off the walls in bursts of chaotic energy.
Sugar Glider
A tiny, wide-eyed marsupial that glides between furniture on stretched membranes of skin.
Iguana
A scaly, prehistoric-looking lizard that basks under heat lamps with regal indifference.
